DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT TREVOR HUBBARD's Demurrer to Amended Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Trevor Hubbard's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. Count 2 adequately pleads a claim for breach of oral contracts - the at-issue loans. (FACC 13:1-16.) Whether Hubbard's personal guarantees were oral is immaterial because the loans were pled to be oral: a "general demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.) Further, the FACC adequately pleads Hubbard and Butchershop Creative, LLC were alter egos. (FAC 3:10-23, 11:5-12:8.) Count 3 adequately pleads fraud with specificity. (Opp. 12:27-14:5.) Justifiable reliance is question of fact not properly decided on demurrer.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS BUTCHERSHOP CREATIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Butchershop Creative, LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. Count 2 adequately pleads a claim for a breach of oral contracts - the at-issue loans. (FACC 13:1-16.) Whether Trevor Hubbard's personal guarantees were oral is immaterial because the loans were oral: a "demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.) Further, the FACC adequately pleads Hubbard and Butchershop were alter egos. (FAC 3:10-23, 11:5-12:8.) Count 3 is pled only against Hubbard (FACC 13:18), so Butchershop lacks standing to demur to it.

Order signed.

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT BUTCHERSHOP CREATIVE, LLC's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained in part with leave to amend. The breach of contract counts fail to specify whether the alleged contracts are written, oral or implied. The demurrer is otherwise overruled.
